title: [cross-file] tx_mutex not protected during lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort()
description:
Interaction: lichen_l2.c:lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort <-> lora_l2.c:lichen_lora_l2_deinit

Bug: lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() only reinitializes rx_mutex (line 1621). But tx_mutex can also be corrupted if:
1. RX thread is aborted while in lora_rx_callback()
2. Callback calls lichen_l2_input()
3. lichen_l2_input() is pure rx_mutex user (no tx_mutex)

Wait - let me check... Actually lichen_l2_input() only acquires rx_mutex (line 1656). The tx_mutex is only used in lichen_l2_send() and lichen_l2_enable(). The RX thread never acquires tx_mutex.

However, the RX callback path (lora_rx_callback -> lichen_l2_input) acquires rx_mutex. If the RX thread is aborted while holding rx_mutex (inside lichen_l2_input), then rx_mutex is corrupted.

The issue: lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() is called from lichen_lora_l2_deinit() AFTER line 874. But lora_l2_deinit() at lines 766-768 acquires tx_buf_mutex and at line 834 reinits lora_mutex. It does NOT check if tx_buf_mutex is corrupted.

Actually tx_buf_mutex is different from tx_mutex (lichen_l2.c). They're separate mutexes in separate modules. lora_l2.c has tx_buf_mutex for TX buffer protection. lichen_l2.c has tx_mutex for tx_ipv6_buf/tx_frame_buf protection.

So the issue is: if the RX thread was aborted while in lichen_l2_input, rx_mutex is corrupted. lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() correctly reinits rx_mutex. But the RX thread doesn't hold tx_mutex, so no issue there.

Actually this is NOT a bug. The code is correct. Withdrawing this finding.
close_reason: False positive: tx_mutex (lichen_l2.c) is separate from tx_buf_mutex (lora_l2.c). RX thread only acquires rx_mutex in lichen_l2_input(), never tx_mutex. tx_mutex is only used by TX path (lichen_l2_send) and enable/disable. RX thread abort cannot corrupt tx_mutex.
